name: Manicotti

ingredients.item: large fresh or packaged manicotti shells
can San Marzano tomatoes plus one can of water

method: First of all, if you are using manicotti that aren’t fresh, then you will need to boil a pot of salted water and cook the manicotti to very al dente. Drain and line them up on a cutting board. Otherwise, if you are using fresh manicotti you can skip the boiling step because they will cook in the sauce. In a frying pan on medium heat, prepare your sauce by adding 2 T of olive oil and crushed garlic, and saute for one minute, then add 1 can of tomatoes, water, 1 t salt, 1 t pepper, and 4 torn basil leaves. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes on low. In the meantime, in a small bowl, add ricotta, parmesan cheese, beaten egg, parsley, 1 t salt and 1 t pepper. Mix well and transfer to a piping bag. In an 8 inch casserole dish put a ladle of sauce on the bottom. Carefully pipe the cheese into each manicotti and arrange them in your dish. Pour sauce over the top and place in a preheated 350°F oven for 30-40 minutes. When done the cheese will be melted and bubbly. Sprinkle with parmesan and garnish with parsley. Serves 2. Serving suggestion fresh bread and salad. Olé
